name: Slice & Bake Chocolate Wafer Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four 14" x 12-inch pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap; set aside.
2. In a bowl, cream butter or margarine, sugar, eggs, and vanilla until light and fluffy.
3. In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking soda, and cocoa powder.
4. Stir the flour mixture into the sugar mixture until evenly distributed.
5. Divide the dough into 4 pieces. Shape each piece into an 8 to 10 inch roll.
6. Wrap each roll in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
7. Place the rolls in a freezer container with a tight-fitting lid, or wrap them airtight in foil.
8. Label and date the container. Store in the freezer. Use within 6 months.
To make cookies:
1. Slightly thaw a roll of dough by letting it sit at room temperature for about an hour.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
3. Lightly grease baking sheets with butter or margarine.
4. Slice the dough into slices or chunks (about 1/2 inch thick).
5. Arrange the slices on the prepared baking sheets, about 1/2 inch apart.
6. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until the cookies are set on the edges and slightly firm on top.
7. Remove the cookies from the oven and cool them on wire racks.
